question: How many personal fouls does it take to foul out of an NBA game?
reference: A player is disqualified from the game when he receives his sixth personal foul.

question: How long is the shot clock in the NBA?
reference: The shot clock shall start at 24 seconds unless otherwise provided in Rule 7.

question: How many timeouts is each team allowed per game?
reference: Each team is entitled to seven charged timeouts during regulation play.

question: How many players are on each team on the court at one time?
reference: Each team shall consist of five players.

question: What is the duration of each quarter in an NBA game?
reference: All periods of regulation play in the NBA will be twelve minutes.

question: How far is the free throw line from the backboard?
reference: The free throw line shall be 15 feet from the plane of the face of the backboard.

question: How many seconds does a team have to advance the ball past half court?
reference: The team on offense must bring the ball across the midcourt line within 8 seconds.

question: What is the penalty for a technical foul in terms of free throws?
reference: A technical foul is the penalty for unsportsmanlike conduct or violations by team members on the floor or seated on the bench.

question: How many challenges does each coach get per game?
reference: Each team is entitled to one Challenge per game. If a team's first Challenge is successful, the team will be awarded a second Challenge.

question: What happens when a player commits a flagrant foul penalty 2?
reference: Any player who is assessed a flagrant foul penalty 2 must be ejected and will be fined a minimum of $2,000.

question: How long is each overtime period in the NBA?
reference: All overtime periods of play will be five minutes.

question: How long is halftime in an NBA game?
reference: Fifteen minutes will be permitted between halves of all games.

question: How far is the three-point line from the basket?
reference: The three-point field goal area has an arc of 23 feet 9 inches from the middle of the basket which intersects parallel lines 3 feet from the sidelines.

question: What is a dribble in the NBA?
reference: A dribble is movement of the ball, caused by a player in control, who throws or taps the ball to the floor.

question: How long does a player have to attempt a free throw?
reference: A free throw attempt must be made within 10 seconds.

question: What is a flagrant foul penalty 1?
reference: If contact committed against a player is interpreted to be unnecessary, a flagrant foul penalty 1 will be assessed. Two free throws shall be attempted and the ball awarded to the offended team on either side of the court at the free throw line extended.

question: What is a flagrant foul penalty 2?
reference: If contact committed against a player is interpreted to be unnecessary and excessive, a flagrant foul penalty 2 will be assessed. Two free throws shall be attempted and the ball awarded to the offended team on either side of the court at the free throw line extended.

question: How many timeouts can a team use in the fourth quarter?
reference: Each team is limited to no more than four timeouts in the fourth period.

question: What is a free throw in basketball?
reference: A free throw is the privilege given a player to score one point by an unhindered attempt for the goal from a position directly behind the free throw line.

question: When must a player be ejected from an NBA game?
reference: A player must be ejected for a punching foul, a fighting foul, deliberately entering the stands, a flagrant foul penalty 2, a second flagrant foul penalty 1, or participation in the game when not on the team's active list.
